title: A Claude Code skill that turns any codebase into a course.
body: Build first, understand later. Tested the codebase-to-course skill by Zara Zhang. As a learning designer, this one got me.

title: Learning starts with copying someone else's process.
body: That's not theft. That's how every skill you've ever built began. Stop gatekeeping titles. Stop gatekeeping knowledge.

title: Five fixes. Not about prompting better. About building a system.
body: Which one are you starting with? Systems thinking, applied to the AI workflow problem most teams keep trying to patch.

title: Most teams added AI on top of the old workflow. Nothing actually got removed.
body: That's why the productivity boost didn't materialize. The legacy steps are still there, and you layered a new one on top.

title: Preparation isn't the opposite of innovation.
body: It's what makes innovation stick. Companies arrive with energy and good intentions, but no plan, no stakeholder alignment, no constraints.

title: Anthropic proved AI has functional emotions. What that means for feedback.
body: Same scenario. Same learner response. Three completely different coaching outputs depending on emotional state.

title: The metrics that actually tell you behavior changed.
body: Completion rate is not on this list. That's the point. Which one does your team actually use?

title: The AI verification burden is real.
body: You saved time writing. Now you're spending it reviewing. The bottleneck just changed locations.

title: Five moves for your next stakeholder conversation.
body: Save this for the next time you're walking into a meeting where no one's aligned. Which one does your team skip most?

title: MD files, skills, agents. AI through an L&D lens.
body: Once it clicks through an L&D lens it makes total sense. Sharing everything as I figure it out.

title: What L&D is actually talking about this week.
body: AI hype fatigue, fake content, a role identity crisis, and the most human skill in our field that nobody is talking about.

title: Most corporate training doesn't change behavior. It just gets completed.
body: There's a difference, and it matters. My name is Emily Green, founder of Caboodle Design.
